prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Spanish  
“El mejor momento para sembrar esperanza fue ayer; el próximo mejor momento es este latido.”  
Note: It keeps the urgency but swaps the literal tree for “hope,” something every listener already values.

Japanese  
「木を植えるなら、幻の二十年前か、確かな今。」  
Note: By calling the past “phantom” and the present “certain,” the saying pushes the hearer off the fence of regret and into action.
